cards.uprightMeaning: You're standing at the edge with nothing but trust in your pocket. The road doesn't need a map — it needs your feet on it. Jump.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're calling it spontaneity, but it's just avoidance wearing a flower crown. Freedom without direction is just drifting.
cards.keywords: leap, trust, beginnings, innocence, open road

cards.uprightMeaning: Everything you need is already on the table in front of you. You've got the skills, the voice, and the moment — now use them before the moment passes.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're running a con and the mark is yourself. All that talent means nothing if you're just performing instead of creating.
cards.keywords: skill, manifestation, resourcefulness, willpower, action

cards.uprightMeaning: The answers aren't in the noise — they're in the silence between songs. Sit with what you already know but haven't admitted yet.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've disconnected from your own knowing and started trusting everyone else's opinion instead. The quiet voice inside got drowned out by the crowd.
cards.keywords: intuition, mystery, inner knowing, stillness, secrets

cards.uprightMeaning: You're in full bloom and the garden is yours. Create without permission, nurture without apology, and let abundance flow like it's supposed to — freely.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've been giving so much there's nothing left for the soil. Generosity that empties you isn't love, it's martyrdom with better aesthetics.
cards.keywords: abundance, nurture, creativity, growth, sensuality

cards.uprightMeaning: Someone's got to build the stage before the band can play. Structure isn't the enemy of freedom — it's the framework that makes freedom possible.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've become the authority you swore you'd never be. Control dressed up as responsibility is still just control.
cards.keywords: structure, authority, discipline, foundation, order

cards.uprightMeaning: There's wisdom in the old ways if you know which parts to keep and which to burn. Find the teacher who earned it through living, not through title.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're following rules written by people who never questioned anything. Tradition without examination is just obedience with a longer history.
cards.keywords: tradition, mentorship, questioning doctrine, shared wisdom, convention

cards.uprightMeaning: This isn't about romance — it's about choosing what you stand with, eyes open, no illusions. Real union means showing up as your whole unedited self.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're staying in something because leaving feels harder than being honest. A bond built on avoidance is just two people being lonely together.
cards.keywords: choice, union, authenticity, alignment, values

cards.uprightMeaning: You've got opposing forces pulling at you and that's exactly the fuel you need. Grab the reins and ride the contradiction — that tension is your engine.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're mistaking aggression for progress. Pushing harder doesn't help when you're pointed at the wrong horizon.
cards.keywords: determination, willpower, momentum, drive, conquest

cards.uprightMeaning: Real power isn't loud — it's the quiet hand that calms the lion instead of caging it. You don't have to fight what's wild in you; just learn its language.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're either white-knuckling your composure or letting the beast run the show. Neither is strength — one is suppression, the other is surrender.
cards.keywords: courage, patience, compassion, inner strength, gentle power

cards.uprightMeaning: Walk away from the noise for a while. Not because you're running, but because the truth you need lives somewhere the crowd can't follow.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've turned solitude into isolation and called it enlightenment. A lantern only works if you eventually bring it back to light someone else's way.
cards.keywords: solitude, introspection, wisdom, withdrawal, inner light

cards.uprightMeaning: The wheel turns whether you like it or not. This is your moment — ride the upswing and don't waste time pretending you earned what luck just handed you.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're blaming bad luck for choices you made with your eyes closed. The wheel didn't put you here — your refusal to move did.
cards.keywords: cycles, change, fate, turning point, fortune

cards.uprightMeaning: The scales don't care about your intentions — they weigh what you actually did. Own your choices and their consequences. That's the only real fairness.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're bending the truth to fit your story. Justice delayed by self-deception isn't justice at all — it's just a longer fall when it catches up.
cards.keywords: truth, accountability, fairness, consequence, balance

cards.uprightMeaning: Stop struggling. The view from upside down shows you everything you missed while you were busy trying to stay upright. Surrender isn't giving up — it's giving in to a bigger picture.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're calling it patience but you're really just stuck. There's a difference between waiting for clarity and being too scared to move.
cards.keywords: surrender, new perspective, pause, sacrifice, letting go

cards.uprightMeaning: Something's ending and it needs to. You can't plant new seeds in a garden that's still full of last year's dead roots. Let it compost.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're keeping something alive that died a long time ago, and the effort is hollowing you out. Let go before it takes more than it already has.
cards.keywords: ending, transformation, release, rebirth, letting die

cards.uprightMeaning: Mix it slow. The best things — music, bread, relationships — need time and the right proportions. You're not compromising; you're alchemizing.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've gone all-in on one thing and starved everything else. Excess disguised as passion is still just excess.
cards.keywords: balance, patience, moderation, alchemy, flow

cards.uprightMeaning: Look at the chains around your neck — they're not locked. You chose this, and you can unchoose it. But first you have to stop pretending it feels good.
cards.reversedMeaning: You broke one chain and called yourself free while wrapping another around your wrist. Liberation is a practice, not a single dramatic exit.
cards.keywords: bondage, shadow, addiction, illusion, materialism

cards.uprightMeaning: The thing you built on a bad foundation is coming down, and it should. You can't renovate a lie — sometimes the only honest thing is rubble.
cards.reversedMeaning: You survived the collapse but you're trying to rebuild the same broken structure. The tower fell for a reason — build something different this time.
cards.keywords: upheaval, revelation, breakdown, liberation, sudden change

cards.uprightMeaning: After the wreckage, there's this — a sky full of something worth believing in again. Pour yourself back into the world. You've earned this tenderness.
cards.reversedMeaning: You lost faith and now you're faking hope because it's what people expect. Cynicism dressed as realism is still just a wound you haven't tended.
cards.keywords: hope, healing, renewal, inspiration, vulnerability

cards.uprightMeaning: Nothing is what it looks like right now and that's okay. Walk through the fog anyway — your instincts know the path even when your eyes don't.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so afraid of being deceived that you've stopped trusting anything, including yourself. Fear of illusion has become its own kind of blindness.
cards.keywords: illusion, fear, intuition, the unconscious, confusion

cards.uprightMeaning: This is the real thing — joy without a price tag, warmth without conditions. Dance in it. You don't have to earn the sunlight; you just have to step outside.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're performing happiness instead of feeling it. That forced smile is costing you more energy than just being honest about what's dimmed.
cards.keywords: joy, vitality, clarity, celebration, authenticity

cards.uprightMeaning: The call is coming from inside you. Everything you've lived through has been leading to this reckoning — not punishment, but the moment you finally answer for who you really are.
cards.reversedMeaning: You heard the call and hit snooze. Self-judgment without action is just guilt on repeat, and guilt never changed anything by itself.
cards.keywords: reckoning, calling, rebirth, absolution, awakening

cards.uprightMeaning: You made it all the way around and the circle is closing. This isn't the end — it's the exhale before the next breath. Take it in. You did the work.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so close to completion but you keep finding reasons not to finish. The last mile is the hardest because it means you'll have to start something new.
cards.keywords: completion, wholeness, integration, fulfillment, journey's end

cards.uprightMeaning: A spark just landed in your hands — raw creative fire with no instructions. Don't plan it to death. Light something with it before it goes out.
cards.reversedMeaning: You had the spark but you smothered it with overthinking. Inspiration doesn't wait for you to feel ready.
cards.keywords: spark, inspiration, new passion, creative fire, potential

cards.uprightMeaning: You're holding the world in your hands and deciding which direction to walk. The comfort zone has walls — step past them and see what's actually out there.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've been standing at the crossroads so long you've put down roots. Planning without moving is just a sophisticated way of staying stuck.
cards.keywords: planning, vision, decision, restlessness, future

cards.uprightMeaning: Your ships are out there and the horizon is answering back. You did the work of launching — now trust the current to carry what you sent.
cards.reversedMeaning: You sent something into the world and now you're micromanaging the ocean. Let it travel or call it back, but stop standing on the shore wringing your hands.
cards.keywords: expansion, foresight, momentum, exploration, progress

cards.uprightMeaning: The stage is set, the people showed up, and the music is good. Celebrate what you've built with the people who helped build it. This gathering is earned.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're throwing a party nobody feels welcome at, or you're too restless to enjoy the one that's happening. Celebration without presence is just noise.
cards.keywords: celebration, community, homecoming, milestone, gathering

cards.uprightMeaning: Everybody's swinging but nobody's landing a real hit. This conflict is creative friction if you use it right — let the clash of ideas sharpen something instead of breaking it.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've turned disagreement into a lifestyle. Fighting for the sake of fighting stopped being punk a long time ago — now it's just exhausting.
cards.keywords: conflict, competition, creative tension, struggle, debate

cards.uprightMeaning: The crowd is cheering and this time it's real — you earned this recognition by doing the thing nobody thought you could. Ride the wave, but remember who held the door open.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're chasing applause instead of meaning. When the validation dries up, will you still believe in what you made?
cards.keywords: victory, recognition, pride, triumph, public praise

cards.uprightMeaning: They're coming at you from all sides but you've got the high ground and you know it. Stand your ground — what you built is worth defending.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so busy fighting off every critic that you've forgotten what you're defending. Not every hill is worth dying on.
cards.keywords: defiance, standing ground, perseverance, challenge, courage

cards.uprightMeaning: Everything is moving fast and for once, that's exactly right. Don't slow down to think — the arrows are already in the air. Ride the momentum.
cards.reversedMeaning: You launched everything at once and now it's all crashing into each other mid-flight. Speed without aim is just beautiful chaos with consequences.
cards.keywords: speed, momentum, swift action, messages, acceleration

cards.uprightMeaning: You're battered and leaning on your last stick but you're still standing. That's not stubbornness — that's the kind of grit that rewrites the story. One more round.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've been fighting so long you forgot you're allowed to rest. Resilience without recovery isn't brave — it's just self-destruction with better PR.
cards.keywords: resilience, persistence, grit, weariness, last stand

cards.uprightMeaning: You picked up every stick on the road and now you can barely see where you're going. Some of those burdens aren't yours — put them down before they break your back.
cards.reversedMeaning: You finally dropped the load but now you feel guilty about it. Letting go of what's crushing you isn't laziness, it's survival.
cards.keywords: burden, overwhelm, responsibility, exhaustion, overcommitment

cards.uprightMeaning: You're the kid with the demo tape and nothing to lose. That fearless curiosity is your superpower — chase the idea before the world teaches you to be cautious.
cards.reversedMeaning: All talk, no tape. You keep announcing projects that never materialize because the daydream is more comfortable than the work.
cards.keywords: enthusiasm, exploration, curiosity, messenger, bold beginnings

cards.uprightMeaning: You're riding hard toward the next adventure with your hair on fire and zero regrets. That fearless energy is magnetic — just make sure you're charging toward something, not away from something.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're burning bridges and calling it liberation. Recklessness isn't the same as courage, and eventually you'll need a bridge you already torched.
cards.keywords: adventure, passion, impulsiveness, boldness, restless energy

cards.uprightMeaning: You walk into the room and the temperature changes. That's not ego — that's the warmth of someone who knows exactly who she is and doesn't dim it for anyone's comfort.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're demanding attention instead of commanding it. When confidence curdles into jealousy or control, the fire stops warming and starts burning.
cards.keywords: confidence, warmth, magnetism, independence, fierce joy

cards.uprightMeaning: You lead by lighting fires in other people, not by sitting on a throne. Vision without ego, ambition without greed — that's the kind of power that actually changes things.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've become the dictator of your own revolution. When the visionary stops listening to the people, the vision becomes just another empire.
cards.keywords: leadership, vision, charisma, bold action, inspired authority

cards.uprightMeaning: Your heart just cracked open and something real is pouring in. Don't analyze it — just let yourself feel the full weight of being alive and connected.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're blocking the flow because the last time you opened up, it hurt. But a sealed cup doesn't protect you — it just makes you thirsty.
cards.keywords: emotional opening, love, compassion, new feeling, overflow

cards.uprightMeaning: Two people seeing each other clearly and choosing to stay anyway. This connection is the real deal — built on mutual respect, not need.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're confusing intensity with intimacy. When the connection becomes about completing each other instead of complementing, it starts to corrode.
cards.keywords: connection, partnership, mutual respect, harmony, bond

cards.uprightMeaning: Raise a glass with the ones who showed up when it mattered. This isn't small talk at a party — it's the kind of joy that only happens when your people are your people.
cards.reversedMeaning: The group has turned into a clique and somebody's getting left out. When celebration becomes exclusive, the joy has curdled into something possessive.
cards.keywords: friendship, celebration, community, joy, belonging

cards.uprightMeaning: You're sitting under a tree ignoring the cup being offered because you're too busy being disappointed by the three you already have. Look up.
cards.reversedMeaning: You finally noticed what was being offered but you're still not sure you want it. At least you're looking — that's the first step out of the sulk.
cards.keywords: apathy, discontent, contemplation, withdrawal, missed opportunity

cards.uprightMeaning: Yeah, three cups spilled. It hurts and you should feel it. But there are two still standing behind you — grief is real, but so is what's left.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're weaponizing your sadness, wearing it like an identity instead of walking through it. Loss is a passage, not an address.
cards.keywords: grief, loss, regret, perspective, what remains

cards.uprightMeaning: Something from your past is reaching out — a memory, a person, a version of yourself you left behind. There's sweetness here if you don't try to live in it permanently.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're living in a nostalgia that never actually existed the way you remember it. The past is a nice place to visit, but you can't set up camp there.
cards.keywords: nostalgia, innocence, memory, reunion, the past

cards.uprightMeaning: Seven beautiful possibilities floating in front of you and at least four of them are mirages. Dream big, but learn to tell the difference between vision and fantasy.
cards.reversedMeaning: You picked one and now you're wondering if you chose wrong. You didn't — you just have to stop looking back at the options that dissolved.
cards.keywords: fantasy, choices, illusion, wishful thinking, imagination

cards.uprightMeaning: You're walking away from something that looks perfectly fine from the outside. But you know it's empty, and staying would cost you more than leaving ever could.
cards.reversedMeaning: You keep almost leaving but turning back at the last second. The fear of the unknown is keeping you in a known misery — and that's no way to live.
cards.keywords: departure, disillusionment, seeking, walking away, deeper meaning

cards.uprightMeaning: You got what you wished for and it actually feels as good as you imagined. Sit with that satisfaction — you don't have to immediately want the next thing.
cards.reversedMeaning: You got everything you wanted and it still feels hollow. Maybe the wish was never really yours — maybe you were chasing someone else's version of enough.
cards.keywords: satisfaction, wish fulfilled, contentment, pleasure, gratitude

cards.uprightMeaning: This is the real commune — not the idea of it, but the messy, beautiful reality of people who chose each other. Emotional abundance shared freely. This is what it was all for.
cards.reversedMeaning: The picture looks perfect but someone in it is pretending. A family or community built on performance instead of truth is just a prettier cage.
cards.keywords: emotional fulfillment, family, harmony, shared joy, togetherness

cards.uprightMeaning: A fish just popped out of your cup and you're not even surprised — that's the magic of staying open. Follow the strange feeling; it's trying to show you something beautiful.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so guarded that wonder can't get through anymore. Somewhere along the way you decided that being open was the same as being naive, and you lost something precious.
cards.keywords: wonder, intuitive message, sensitivity, creative stirring, openness

cards.uprightMeaning: You're riding toward something your heart won't let you ignore. Follow that emotional truth — it knows things your rational mind hasn't caught up with yet.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've turned romance into performance and feelings into a script. When the grand gesture replaces genuine emotion, you're just acting in your own movie.
cards.keywords: romance, idealism, following the heart, charm, emotional quest

cards.uprightMeaning: You hold space for everyone's pain without drowning in it. That's a rare gift — emotional depth with boundaries, compassion without self-erasure.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've absorbed so much of everyone else's feelings that you can't find your own anymore. Empathy without boundaries isn't kindness — it's codependence.
cards.keywords: emotional depth, empathy, intuition, nurturing, boundaries

cards.uprightMeaning: You've been through the emotional deep end and came back fluent in feelings. Now you lead with your heart without being ruled by it — that's emotional mastery.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're using emotional intelligence as a tool for manipulation instead of connection. A calm exterior hiding a controlling interior is just a more sophisticated kind of dishonesty.
cards.keywords: emotional maturity, calm authority, diplomacy, wisdom, composure

cards.uprightMeaning: A single clear thought cutting through all the noise. This is your moment of mental breakthrough — speak the truth while it's sharp enough to matter.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've got the truth but you're wielding it like a weapon instead of a tool. Clarity without compassion is just cruelty with good aim.
cards.keywords: clarity, truth, breakthrough, mental force, new idea

cards.uprightMeaning: You've blindfolded yourself to avoid making the choice, but the swords are still in your hands. Indecision is a decision — and it's the worst one available.
cards.reversedMeaning: The blindfold came off and now you wish it hadn't. The truth was always there; you were just protecting yourself from having to act on it.
cards.keywords: indecision, avoidance, stalemate, denial, difficult choice

cards.uprightMeaning: The heart got pierced and there's no way around the pain — only through it. This hurt is honest, and honest pain is the beginning of real healing.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're picking at the wound instead of letting it close. Replaying the betrayal on loop doesn't protect you — it just keeps the blade twisting.
cards.keywords: heartbreak, painful truth, sorrow, betrayal, necessary pain

cards.uprightMeaning: Lay down your weapons and rest. This isn't defeat — it's the strategic retreat that lets you come back whole. Even revolutionaries need to sleep.
cards.reversedMeaning: You jumped back into the fight before your wounds healed and now you're bleeding through the bandages. Rest isn't optional — it's the work.
cards.keywords: rest, recovery, retreat, contemplation, stillness

cards.uprightMeaning: You won the argument but lost the people. Collecting other people's swords doesn't make you right — it just makes you alone on a battlefield.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're finally seeing that the victory wasn't worth the cost. Walking back to apologize is harder than winning ever was, and infinitely more brave.
cards.keywords: hollow victory, conflict, defeat, pride, walking away

cards.uprightMeaning: You're leaving troubled waters behind and the crossing is quiet but necessary. You're not running — you're choosing a shore where you can finally breathe.
cards.reversedMeaning: You keep circling back to the same rough water because familiar pain feels safer than unknown peace. The boat is ready — stop rowing in circles.
cards.keywords: transition, moving on, passage, healing journey, calmer waters

cards.uprightMeaning: Sometimes you have to be clever instead of confrontational. This isn't about dishonesty — it's about knowing when to take what you need and slip out before the system notices.
cards.reversedMeaning: Your shortcuts are catching up with you. The clever workaround was actually just avoiding the hard conversation, and now the bill is due.
cards.keywords: strategy, stealth, shortcuts, resourcefulness, cunning

cards.uprightMeaning: You're blindfolded and surrounded by swords, but your hands are barely tied. Most of these walls are thoughts you built yourself — question them and they dissolve.
cards.reversedMeaning: You see the trap now but you're still standing in it. Awareness without action is just a more informed kind of imprisonment.
cards.keywords: mental trap, self-imposed limits, restriction, victimhood, paralysis

cards.uprightMeaning: Three a.m. and the thoughts won't stop circling. The anxiety feels like truth but it's just fear wearing logic's clothes. Talk to someone when the sun comes up.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've been carrying this dread so long it started to feel normal. It's not normal — it's a wound that needs daylight, not another sleepless night.
cards.keywords: anxiety, nightmares, worry, dread, mental anguish

cards.uprightMeaning: Ten swords in the back and you're face down. This is rock bottom, and that's actually useful information — the only direction from here is up. The worst is over.
cards.reversedMeaning: You survived the worst but you keep telling the story like it's still happening. At some point the pain becomes a narrative you're choosing to repeat.
cards.keywords: rock bottom, betrayal, endings, defeat, painful conclusion

cards.uprightMeaning: You're sharp, curious, and asking the questions nobody wants to hear. Keep that edge — the world needs people who refuse to accept answers that don't add up.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're using your intelligence to cut people down instead of cutting through problems. A sharp tongue without wisdom is just cruelty with a vocabulary.
cards.keywords: curiosity, mental agility, questioning, vigilance, truth-seeking

cards.uprightMeaning: You're charging at the truth with everything you've got and nothing will slow you down. That intellectual ferocity changes things — just make sure you're not cutting down allies on the way.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've turned your mind into a battering ram and now everything looks like a wall. Not every situation needs your full force — sometimes it needs your full attention.
cards.keywords: intellectual charge, ambition, direct action, haste, sharp mind

cards.uprightMeaning: You see through every lie and you're not afraid to say so. That clarity earned through hard experience is a gift — wield it with the precision it deserves.
cards.reversedMeaning: Your standards have become walls and your discernment has become judgment. There's a thin line between seeing clearly and seeing only flaws.
cards.keywords: perceptiveness, independence, clear boundaries, hard-won wisdom, direct speech

cards.uprightMeaning: You make the hard calls with a clear head and a clean conscience. Intellectual authority earned through fairness, not force — that's the kind of mind people actually trust.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've become so rational you've cut yourself off from everything that makes decisions human. A mind without heart isn't wise — it's just cold.
cards.keywords: intellectual authority, fairness, clear judgment, analytical power, ethical mind

cards.uprightMeaning: A seed of real, tangible opportunity just landed in your hand. This isn't a dream — it's something you can build, grow, and live on. Plant it where the soil is good.
cards.reversedMeaning: You let a solid opportunity slip because it didn't look glamorous enough. Not everything worth building announces itself with fireworks.
cards.keywords: opportunity, prosperity, new venture, seed, material beginning

cards.uprightMeaning: You're juggling two things and somehow making it look easy. That's not luck — it's adaptability. Keep the rhythm, stay loose, and don't let anyone add a third ball.
cards.reversedMeaning: The juggling act just became a dropping act. You can't balance what you refuse to prioritize — something's got to give before you do.
cards.keywords: balance, juggling, adaptability, flux, prioritizing

cards.uprightMeaning: The best work happens when the architect, the builder, and the dreamer are all in the same room. Collaborate with people whose skills fill your gaps — ego builds nothing lasting.
cards.reversedMeaning: Nobody's listening to each other and the project is suffering for it. When collaboration turns into competition, the craft dies first.
cards.keywords: collaboration, craftsmanship, teamwork, skill, shared purpose

cards.uprightMeaning: You're holding on so tight your knuckles are white. Security is important, but when it becomes hoarding, you've traded freedom for a fortress nobody can enter — including you.
cards.reversedMeaning: You finally loosened your grip and it's terrifying. But money and resources were meant to flow, not to be clenched in a fist.
cards.keywords: control, hoarding, security, possessiveness, scarcity mindset

cards.uprightMeaning: You're out in the cold and the warmth is right there behind the door you're too proud to knock on. Hardship is real, but isolation in hardship is a choice. Ask for help.
cards.reversedMeaning: The worst is passing and you're starting to see the door again. Recovery from rock bottom starts with one knock — one honest admission that you can't do this alone.
cards.keywords: hardship, exclusion, poverty, isolation, asking for help

cards.uprightMeaning: Give because you can, not because it makes you feel powerful. Real generosity doesn't keep score — it flows downhill to where it's needed most.
cards.reversedMeaning: Your giving has strings attached and everyone can feel them. Generosity with conditions is just leverage wearing a kind face.
cards.keywords: generosity, sharing, charity, reciprocity, giving and receiving

cards.uprightMeaning: You've been tending this garden for a long time and you're tired. The harvest isn't here yet but the growth is real — patience now is an act of faith in your own labor.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're ready to rip out the whole garden because it's not growing fast enough. Impatience will destroy in a day what took months to cultivate.
cards.keywords: patience, assessment, long-term effort, waiting, investment

cards.uprightMeaning: Head down, hands busy, making the same thing over and over until it's perfect. This isn't drudgery — it's devotion. The craft rewards the ones who keep showing up.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're going through the motions but the love left the work a while ago. Perfectionism without passion is just a well-polished cage.
cards.keywords: craftsmanship, dedication, mastery, practice, diligence

cards.uprightMeaning: You built this garden with your own hands and now you get to enjoy it. Self-sufficiency that came from real work — not inherited privilege, but earned independence.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've got the beautiful garden but you're lonely in it. Independence taken too far becomes its own kind of prison — let someone in.
cards.keywords: self-sufficiency, abundance, independence, luxury, earned comfort

cards.uprightMeaning: This is the legacy — not just money, but the values, the stories, the community you leave behind. Build something that outlasts you and nourishes the ones who come next.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so focused on the inheritance that you forgot to live. Legacy means nothing if the people you're leaving it to don't actually know who you were.
cards.keywords: legacy, inheritance, family wealth, permanence, tradition

cards.uprightMeaning: You're holding a coin and studying it like it holds the secrets of the universe. That beginner's devotion to learning the practical stuff is worth more than any diploma — stay hungry.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so busy studying the plan that you never start building. At some point you have to put down the book and pick up the hammer.
cards.keywords: study, apprenticeship, diligence, practical learning, ambition

cards.uprightMeaning: You're the slowest rider on the field and that's exactly why you'll get there. Steady, reliable, and methodical — you finish what everyone else abandoned when it got boring.
cards.reversedMeaning: You've turned reliability into rigidity and patience into paralysis. Being thorough is a virtue; being immovable is just stubbornness with a work ethic.
cards.keywords: reliability, methodical work, persistence, routine, steady progress

cards.uprightMeaning: You make the practical beautiful and the mundane sacred. A home-cooked meal, a hand-built shelf, a community fed and sheltered — that's your kind of magic.
cards.reversedMeaning: You're so focused on providing for everyone else that you've neglected your own foundation. Caretaking that drains you isn't sustainable — it's just slow erosion.
cards.keywords: practical nurturing, resourcefulness, comfort, grounded care, abundance

cards.uprightMeaning: You built something real and lasting without selling your soul to do it. Wealth that serves the community, success that stays rooted — that's the kind of prosperity worth having.
cards.reversedMeaning: The empire you built is starting to own you instead of the other way around. When material success becomes your identity, you've traded your freedom for a fancier cage.
cards.keywords: prosperity, stewardship, material mastery, generosity, grounded success
